abstract: This paper provides a philosophical exploration into the ontological foundations of the educational thought of TGKH. M. Zainuddin Abdul Madjid, specifically concerning the development of Islamic boarding schools (pesantren). Beyond analyzing the essence of his ideas, the study evaluates the contemporary relevance of his pedagogical views within the broader landscape of the Indonesian educational system. Utilizing a qualitative descriptive methodology backed by a rigorous descriptive analysis approach, the research reveals that TGKH. M. Zainuddin Abdul Madjid fundamentally transformed the religious and social fabric of the Lombok community. Through the integration of visionary concepts and practical dedication, he established a robust educational framework that not only facilitated a deep understanding of Islamic teachings among the people of Lombok but also laid a lasting foundation for spiritual and intellectual guidance.
